notes: RE-ASSESSED after PR #1171 (was FROZEN/incapable). codex now has an HTTP hook channel: POST /api/v1/hooks/codex → PermissionRequest → HandlePermissionHook → PermissionPending=true → state_classifier.go rule 0 → waiting (Stop → ready; PostToolUse clears pending). Under `-a untrusted -s read-only` a model-proposed write escalates to a blocking approval overlay (a live TUI state, never written to the rollout JSONL) and the hook drives working→waiting. LIVE-CONFIRMED on codex 0.144.4. Presession adapter: proc-<PID> presession born ready, then the rollout session reconciles with a distinct session_id — every post-birth phase anchors to the REAL session, never the presession row. Recipe holds in waiting (never approves), so final state = waiting, mirroring the claudecode sibling. RECORDING DEFERRED: the hook POSTs to hardcoded http://localhost:7837, so the :7838 coexist recorder can't capture it — record against a :7837 --record daemon via `of record run --attach`.
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Tool gate via permission prompt

text: Pre-session (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (distinct session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
User prompt drives ready → working on the rollout session
The model-proposed write opens a blocking approval overlay; the PermissionRequest hook sets PermissionPending=true → state_classifier rule 0 → working → waiting (hook-driven, no rollout trace needed)
The waiting episode is user-visible and persists while the approval overlay is held — the recipe never approves, so it does not skip over to ready before teardown
